data.narrative:
Cohere is a Series D AI & Software company founded by Aidan Gomez, Ivan Zhang, and Nick Frosst, currently valued at $7.0B on $1.6B raised. The valuation assessment draws from a peer universe of 9 comparable companies—1 public (Palantir) and 8 private, including same-stage AI & Software benchmarks: Anysphere ($29.3B), Wayve ($8.6B), Hugging Face ($4.5B), and Hive AI ($2.0B).

Two methods triangulate the valuation. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 1.5x step-up multiplier to the $7.0B last post-money valuation, implying $10.5B. Peer-median analysis across the four same-sector, same-stage comparables yields $6.5B, with the peer cohort ranging from $2.0B to $29.3B. The wide range reflects heterogeneous valuations within the AI frontier-tech space at Series D.

Synthesizing both methods produces a triangulated range of $5.6B (low) to $12.1B (high), with a median of $8.5B. The current $7.0B valuation sits slightly below this midpoint. Key uncertainties include the significant dispersion in peer valuations—a 14.6x spread between Hive AI and Anysphere—and the limited precedent for public-market comparables, with only Palantir available. Valuation sensitivity remains high to assumptions about TAM realization and capital efficiency relative to the peer set.
